title: Stop Letting Others Define Your Worth
subtitle: 别让他人定义你的价值

paragraphs.en: Lao Zi once said, 'Care about what other people think and you will always be their prisoner.' People-pleasing, or seeking self-worth through others' approval, is unproductive and an exhausting way to go through life. Why do we allow what others think of us to have so much power over how we feel about ourselves?
paragraphs.zh: 老子曾言：“在意别人的看法，你将永远是他们的囚徒。”讨好他人，或通过他人认可寻求自我价值，是一种徒劳且令人疲惫的生活方式。为何我们允许别人对我们的看法如此左右我们对自己的感受？

paragraphs.en: If it's true that you can't please all people all of the time, wouldn't it make sense to stop trying? Unfortunately, sense often isn't driving our behavior. For social beings who desire love and belonging, wanting to be liked, and caring about the effect we have on others, is healthy and allows us to make connections.
paragraphs.zh: 如果你确实无法时刻取悦所有人，停止尝试难道不合理吗？不幸的是，理性往往并非我们行为的驱动力。对于渴望爱与归属感的社交动物来说，想被喜欢、在乎对他人的影响是健康的，这有助于我们建立联系。

paragraphs.en: However, where we get into trouble is when our self-worth is dependent upon whether we win someone's approval or not. This need to be liked can be traced back to when we were children and were completely dependent on others to take care of us. Small children are not just learning how to walk and communicate, they are also trying to learn how the world works.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，当我们的自我价值取决于是否赢得他人认可时，麻烦就来了。这种被喜欢的需要可追溯至童年时期，那时我们完全依赖他人照顾。小孩子不仅在学习走路和交流，也在努力理解世界如何运作。

paragraphs.en: We learn about who we are and what is expected of us based on interactions with others, so, to a four-year-old, if Mommy or Daddy doesn't like him or her, there is the danger that they will abandon them. We need to understand that when we desperately want someone to approve of us, it's being driven by that little kid part of us that is still terrified of abandonment.
paragraphs.zh: 我们通过与他人的互动了解自己是谁以及别人对我们的期望，因此对一个四岁孩子而言，若妈妈或爸爸不喜欢自己，就存在被抛弃的危险。我们须明白，当我们极度渴望某人认可时，驱使我们的是内心深处那个仍害怕被抛弃的小孩。

paragraphs.en: As you become more capable of providing yourself with the approval you seek, your need for external validation will start to vanish, leaving you stronger, more confident, and yes, happier in your life. Imagine how much time we lose each moment we restrain our authentic selves in an effort to be liked.
paragraphs.zh: 随着你越来越有能力给予自己所需的认可，你对外部认可的需求将开始消失，让你变得更坚强、更自信，生活也更幸福。想象一下，每当我们为讨人喜欢而压抑真实的自我时，浪费了多少时间。

paragraphs.en: If we base our worth on the opinions of others, we cheat ourselves of the power to shape our experiences and embrace life not only for others but also for ourselves, because ultimately, there is no difference. So embrace the cliché and love yourself as it's highly doubtful that you'll regret it.
paragraphs.zh: 若将自我价值建立在他人的看法之上，我们便剥夺了自己塑造经历、拥抱生活的力量——不仅为他人，也为自己，因为归根结底二者并无区别。所以拥抱那句老话，爱自己吧，你绝不会后悔。

words.meaning: n. 陈词滥调，老生常谈
